untranslatable_reason: This is a STOP PLACEMENT rule derived from statistical analysis (MAE), not a position sizing rule. It prescribes WHERE to place stops based on historical adverse movement percentiles, not HOW MUCH to risk or SIZE positions. Size would be calculated separately using the MAE-derived stop distance with any of the standard sizing methods (R_MULTIPLE, FIXED_FRACTIONAL, etc.). The rule itself contains no sizing multipliers, equity percentages, or position scaling mechanics.
